fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Tennessee Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Tennessee — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license tennessee, dental assistant certification tennessee, RDA tennessee, DANB CDA tennessee, dental assistant radiography tennessee

license_types.title: Registered Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Tennessee.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: You must graduate from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or complete on-the-job training.

steps.title: Pass the Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) Exam
steps.description: Successfully pass the Tennessee Registered Dental Assistant (RDA) examination.

steps.title: Apply for Registration
steps.description: Submit your application and required documents to the Tennessee Board of Dentistry.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the Tennessee Board of Dentistry by registration.

faqs.question: Does Tennessee require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Tennessee requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Tennessee.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Tennessee?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Tennessee?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $75. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the Tennessee Board of Dentistry by registration.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Tennessee
hero.dek: Tennessee Dental Assistant registration requires graduation from a CODA-accredited dental assisting program or completion of on-the-job training and passage of the RDA exam.
